123086.explanation: This is not desirable for a sports hall, but for a green area we see fewer problems.

123088.explanation: The agreements are already established in the land use change policy for agricultural businesses (vacant agricultural buildings). We align with the policy of the FoodValley region.

123089.explanation: The municipality facilitates this with a sustainability loan at a favorable interest rate, supplemented by other allowances made available by the government.

123092.explanation: Employment agencies and companies that bring labor migrants to our region must ensure decent housing. We are happy to think along and look together for appropriate solutions. This prevents nuisance and ensures that labor migrants do not end up in vulnerable or undesirable situations.

123093.explanation: In Scherpenzeel we have a tree ordinance. For protected trees there is a replanting obligation, and we follow that policy.

123096.explanation: It is not the municipality’s task, but we can facilitate it. However, if the municipality were to finance this, it would always come at the expense of other expenditures.

123097.explanation: The current scheme suffices, and offering it for free would lead to an increase in waste taxes and also reduce the incentive to give items a second life if possible.

123103.explanation: We have realized two new school buildings in Scherpenzeel. The construction of a new public school is in preparation. Maintenance is carried out according to the municipality’s maintenance plan, with a focus on upkeep rather than expansion or improvement.

123104.explanation: The municipality annually contributes to support music education in primary schools. In addition, there is a scheme within the social domain for families who find music lessons too expensive.

123108.explanation: The enforcement powers of the police and public order officers (BOAs) are limited but must be used optimally. Preventive searches and regular patrols in known locations are among the options.

123109.explanation: This is only possible when necessary to maintain public order. The mayor takes the lead here. With current legislation, this is already possible.

123111.explanation: There is a starter scheme and we are happy with that.

123117.explanation: There is a scheme and it must be maintained.

123120.explanation: We strive for safe, well-planned charging spots that fit into the neighborhood and take future growth into account. However, this must not come at the expense of available free parking spaces.

123121.explanation: Inside the built-up area, 30 km/h will be the standard where traffic safety allows this.

123123.explanation: The complete picture plays a role here. It’s not just about finances but also quality.

123127.explanation: We only expand when there is demonstrable need. ’t Zwarteland II is a good example, focused on space for local entrepreneurs.

123130.explanation: Enforcement of the current policy to prevent untidiness.

123137.explanation: If there is significant demand among our residents, we will focus on targeted expansion of social rental housing, fitting the scale and capacity of the village. For comparison: within the FoodValley region, social rental housing accounts for 26%.

123142.explanation: We first focus on Scherpenzeel-South and the available infill locations. Later, housing construction north of the ring road will follow. We build step by step based on actual need.

123143.explanation: Entertainment is an important part of a vibrant village.

123085.explanation: Complex considerations must be made in politics. Referendums suggest that a political choice can be summarized in a simple yes-or-no question. Therefore, referendums do not take the complexity of decisions into account.

123086.explanation: Residents should not be made responsible for the sports hall at De Breehoek. Residents can contribute to green maintenance, but only in consultation with the municipality.

123088.explanation: A careful consideration must be made regarding ancillary activities in the rural area. It can be allowed, but only according to the criteria of, among others, the established land use change policy which also protects the possibilities of existing businesses in the vicinity.

123089.explanation: The municipality cannot simply provide financial support. There is a scheme for future-proof living where loans are provided. If money is made available by the national or provincial government, that is fine.

123092.explanation: At the scale of Scherpenzeel, customized solutions are possible and on-site housing is not the only option.

123093.explanation: This should not apply to every tree. Normal maintenance or removal of any random self-seeded tree or removal of a tree on private property is not a problem. However, it may apply as a rule for visually significant tree structures.

123096.explanation: Attention must always go to limiting (illegal) fireworks and their nuisance, in connection with national legislation that bans consumer fireworks from 2026.

123097.explanation: A fee may be charged for the collection of bulky waste. However, residents should not have to pay an excessive price.

123103.explanation: If required according to maintenance plans, the municipality must do this. It requires transparent and good mutual consultation between the municipality and the school boards.

123104.explanation: This should not apply indiscriminately to everyone, regardless of financial situation.

123108.explanation: The current staffing is sufficient. If expansion is needed, funds must be made available for that, even if it requires cutbacks elsewhere! The number of available community officers must not be skimped on. In cooperation with the BOAs, the safety of Scherpenzeel can be well guaranteed.

123109.explanation: More camera surveillance is desired in places where there is nuisance and insecurity.

123111.explanation: Fortunately, Scherpenzeel already has a starter loan and we must keep it that way.

123117.explanation: The existing scheme is rarely used and is therefore a good tool in emergencies.

123120.explanation: The municipality must consider expanding the number of charging points, but this must not come at the expense of existing parking spaces. The necessity of expansion also depends on demand.

123121.explanation: This is already the case in Scherpenzeel and that is a good thing.

123123.explanation: It’s not only about price, but also about quality and identity. High-quality care that matches the identity of the care recipient is of great importance.

123125.explanation: It is a levy whose proceeds can partly be used to counteract the nuisance caused by dog fouling.

123127.explanation: The planning for the expansion of the industrial area is progressing, which is a good thing. There must be space for the expansion of and new establishment of local businesses. A good entrepreneurial climate strengthens communities and is crucial for maintaining facilities.

123130.explanation: At this moment, disposing of a few types of waste is free, and for most waste types it is justified to ask for a contribution. The prices must remain acceptable.

123137.explanation: The basis for new homes to be built should be the needs assessment, which is elaborated in the local housing vision. Starter homes, affordable rental and owner-occupied homes also form an important share.

123142.explanation: In the medium term, space must be made for housing on the north side of De Dreef. To offer a future to the youth who want to continue living in Scherpenzeel. Controlled growth is necessary for the vitality of our village and the preservation of facilities.

123143.explanation: There are plenty of parties, especially in the summer months. Expansion of these is undesirable, among others because of the experienced noise nuisance and sometimes heavy alcohol and drug use.

123085.explanation: We are in favor of a consultative referendum. However, we support a threshold for citizens to request a referendum. This is fixed in the current regulation.

123086.explanation: It encourages clutter. Citizens may of course always participate, but the municipality remains responsible.

123088.explanation: However, on a small scale and fitting with the surroundings and function of the farm business.

123089.explanation: As far as we are concerned, in the form of an interest-free loan. Citizens also have their own responsibility.

123092.explanation: The employer of the seasonal worker is responsible for decent housing and must ensure that no exploitation takes place. This can concern both a company and an employment agency.

123093.explanation: Scherpenzeel has a green character overall. This must certainly remain so, but simply planting a new tree for every one cut down is too simplistic.

123096.explanation: If it is feasible, we consider it a good replacement for consumer fireworks.

123097.explanation: This prevents illegal dumping of bulky waste in rural areas.

123103.explanation: The school buildings are up-to-date and the only outdated building is already being replaced by new construction.

123104.explanation: We want to investigate whether a return to the old subsidy scheme is possible. The current one seems to yield too few new members for the associations.

123108.explanation: In principle, we support this as long as it is not a replacement for police presence.

123109.explanation: We are a small-scale village where many people still know each other. As far as we're concerned, camera surveillance adds little to this.

123111.explanation: We already have a starter loan and it must remain.

123117.explanation: We also believe that under certain circumstances it should be possible for self-employed persons without personnel (zzp’ers).

123120.explanation: More and more people are driving increasingly (partially) electric; we need to keep up with that.

123121.explanation: A number of main roads with separated bike paths may remain at 50 km/h; the rest may be or should become 30 km/h.

123123.explanation: The price is indeed important, but quality must ultimately be decisive.

123125.explanation: Why make only dog owners pay?

123127.explanation: As the CDA, we have opposed Zwarte Land II. We are still not in favor, but if there is a great need, we want to consider it under certain conditions. We do consider it very important that social organizations are also involved.

123130.explanation: We want to encourage citizens to reduce their residual waste.

123137.explanation: Currently, as Scherpenzeel, we already have a low share of social rental housing. We are even in favor of 30% in new housing projects.

123142.explanation: For now, first develop the existing locations, but in the future it cannot be ruled out that land 'over the Dreef' will need to be sought.

123143.explanation: Parties in the village strengthen social cohesion and contribute to the village feeling.

123085.explanation: We are not in favor of referenda. The problem with referenda is that they reduce complex problems to a simple 'yes' or 'no'. As a result, people can only wholly reject or fully embrace a proposal. Only in exceptional cases a consultative referendum.

123086.explanation: In favor. But in practice, this turns out not to be as simple as it sounds. Because if residents move, for example, they will themselves have to organize continued management.

123088.explanation: Agreed, provided this fits within the agreed policy. The agreements are fixed in the policy on change of use for agricultural businesses (Vacant Agricultural Buildings). This policy applies throughout the Foodvalley region.

123089.explanation: This can then be in the form of a loan to enable the necessary investments if residents themselves cannot take it on.

123092.explanation: But providing housing may make it more attractive for seasonal workers to work for that employer. We can imagine that many employers will therefore want to organize this, whether or not on their own business premises.

123093.explanation: We adhere to the policy on greenery and the protected tree structure in the municipality.

123096.explanation: Provided that space can be found in the budget. There is not currently, so a fireworks show will come at the expense of something else.

123097.explanation: Bulky waste can be collected at home for a fee or brought to the Recycling Center. This is a fine arrangement based on the 'polluter pays' principle.

123103.explanation: School buildings must be maintained according to a multi-year maintenance plan. If a school building meets the requirements, no extra money needs to be invested to improve it further.

123108.explanation: Enforcement ensures safety, for example by enforcing vehicle speeds in the center. This is especially important if the number of inhabitants grows in the coming years.

123109.explanation: Cameras provide an increased feeling of safety and help in tracking down, for example, burglars.

123111.explanation: Scherpenzeel has an existing scheme that makes it possible for starters to borrow under favorable conditions.

123117.explanation: Except when people truly face financial difficulties, everyone in principle must pay taxes.

123120.explanation: Charging stations for electric cars have been installed in the municipality, but no fast chargers yet. Fast chargers are being placed in the city center, allowing cars to be fully charged after shopping. This doesn't have to come at the expense of fuel-powered cars.

123121.explanation: 30 km/h where possible. But the local situation should always be considered to determine the most appropriate speed. Certain access routes to the village with separate bike paths can remain at 50 km/h according to the VVD, and the ring road at 80 km/h.

123123.explanation: In our opinion, the entire picture must be taken into account, with price playing an important role.

123127.explanation: We want to efficiently use space on existing business parks and create room for small-scale entrepreneurs. Large companies are better suited near highways. Our preference is to establish a large regional business park in the Foodvalley region where there is sufficient space.

123130.explanation: Except where payment is already required now, such as for bulky waste.

123137.explanation: There is demand for owner-occupied homes in Scherpenzeel. We build according to the need, so that first-time buyers, young people, and elderly in our village can find a home.

123142.explanation: Housing is simply needed. That means we must build. We prefer to sacrifice as little green as possible (within the built-up area and building upwards more), but we won't be able to avoid this entirely.

123143.explanation: The events organized make Scherpenzeel a lively village. The number is currently not so large that it needs to be reduced.

123085.explanation: There is an advisory referendum, which residents have the right to if a large percentage of them desire it.

123086.explanation: We welcome citizens' initiatives; we consider this important for community spirit.

123088.explanation: Provided it fits within the environmental plan.

123089.explanation: We want residents to be supported in making their own homes and neighborhoods more sustainable.

123096.explanation: It is not for the municipality to organize, but it can be facilitated.

123111.explanation: We want residents to be able to continue living in the place where they already live.

123123.explanation: We find the quality/price ratio more important than price alone.

123130.explanation: The polluter pays, except for scrap metal, chemical waste, electrical appliances, PMD (plastic, metal, drink cartons), diapers, and paper.

123137.explanation: Scherpenzeel is currently around 17%, it is important that many social rental homes are built. The new council must prioritize this.

123085.explanation: The municipal council is elected by our residents, and they are then responsible for turning words into action. Participation is important, as is prior consultation. We as council members must listen carefully.

123086.explanation: That works well for a while, but during vacations and illness there is no continuity; this may remain with the municipality.

123088.explanation: Farming and recreation go well together. It's good that the role and contribution of farmers to our country are recognized. The scale of Scherpenzeel does not allow for many small campings, but a few can certainly be added.

123089.explanation: To a limited extent, more as a startup subsidy to get a project going. The responsibility lies with the residents.

123092.explanation: The pressure on housing in our village is enormous. Labor migrants already occupy too many homes that Scherpenzeel residents could use. Employers must therefore provide their own housing. This does not have to be on their own grounds but preferably centrally with other employers.

123093.explanation: The greener, the better for our residents, especially with the increasingly warm climate. Trees cool and absorb CO2.

123096.explanation: Enjoying together for community spirit. Safe and fun!

123097.explanation: Costs are too high; as it is now arranged via Remondis, that works fine.

123103.explanation: There must be a ten-year plan for school housing. Especially with the upcoming growth and in the new neighborhood, a school is needed. Not to overcrowd the Glashorst School and Korenmaat further.

123104.explanation: Music contributes to the good development of (young) children.

123108.explanation: There are enough Enforcement Officers (BOAs), but enforcement is insufficient. Enforcement means being on the street and acting visibly. This must improve and be more consistent.

123109.explanation: At certain locations, this is definitely allowed. Drug dealing is increasing, as is the feeling of insecurity.

123111.explanation: Good plan and has worked well before.

123117.explanation: No, compensation has already been arranged for taxes and there are other good schemes for residents with low income. These must be maintained.

123120.explanation: Often charging can already be done at work or at home. People also typically charge only once or twice a week. We don't want a special status for electric cars. Parking spaces are already scarce enough.

123121.explanation: That is already a fact, so it is already arranged.

123123.explanation: Agree, price is important but can never come at the expense of quality.

123125.explanation: No, unfortunately we need this money and use it to cover costs for cleanup, placing bins, and prune bags.

123127.explanation: That is allowed, but only small Scherpenzeel companies. Zwarte Land II is in the startup phase.

123130.explanation: Definitely, it prevents it from ending up in other waste and is good for our living environment.

123137.explanation: We are far behind, so we must get to work. Fortunately, we can now build these homes for Scherpenzeel residents. The law has changed so a large percentage is now reserved for Scherpenzeel residents.

123142.explanation: Yes, in the long term. But first infill in the center, where there is space. Also consult with Woonstede to collaborate.

123143.explanation: As things are now, it is well organized. The events regulation was recently amended and everyone now has their place.

statements.title: Houses should be built north of the ring road around Scherpenzeel.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The residents of Scherpenzeel should be able to express their opinion on important issues via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Scherpenzeel moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
For every tree cut down in the municipality, a new one must be planted.
Residents should be able to take over the management of, for example, a sports hall or a public green space from the municipality.
Scherpenzeel should have more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) focused on public safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should invest money in improving all school buildings during the next council term.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet in de komende

titleParts.glossary: The municipal council is elected for a period of four years.

titleParts.text: geld steken in het verbeteren van alle schoolgebouwen.

statements.title: Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: Dropping off waste at the recycling centre should remain free of charge.

titleParts.text: Het inleveren van afval bij de

titleParts.glossary: A place where citizens and businesses can bring different kinds of waste.

statements.title: Scherpenzeel should have more camera surveillance.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
The municipality should stop forgiving municipal taxes for low-income residents.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet stoppen met het

titleParts.glossary: That people no longer have to pay for something.
Taxes you have to pay to the municipality (e.g., sewer charge, waste disposal charge, dog tax).

titleParts.text: voor inwoners met een laag inkomen.

statements.title: First-time homebuyers should be able to receive a starter loan from the municipality.

titleParts.text: Mensen die voor het eerst een huis willen kopen, moeten van de gemeente een

titleParts.glossary: An extra loan on top of your mortgage, which you can only get when buying a house for the first time.

statements.title: Every primary school child should be able to receive music lessons subsidized by the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Money the government gives to citizens, businesses, and organizations to support them.

titleParts.text: van de gemeente muziekles kunnen krijgen.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the street.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: The business parks in Scherpenzeel should be allowed to expand.
The municipality should abolish the dog tax.

titleParts.glossary: Tax paid to the municipality for owning one or more dogs.

statements.title: At least 25% of the newly built homes in Scherpenzeel should be social rental housing. In the Netherlands, 28% of homes are currently social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Scherpenzeel moet minimaal 25% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: Fewer events should be organized in public spaces.

titleParts.text: Er moeten minder feesten worden georganiseerd in de

titleParts.glossary: Outdoor areas that are accessible to everyone, such as streets, squares, and parks.
